observation_summary: The Base32 codec throws an IllegalArgumentException when using 'W' as a padding character with the hex alphabet, even though 'W' should be a valid padding character.
hypothesis: The HEX_DECODE_TABLE in the Base32 class contains an incorrect entry (the value 32) that maps a character to a valid Base32 value, causing the alphabet validation logic to incorrectly identify 'W' as being part of the alphabet.
prediction: Removing the erroneous value 32 from the HEX_DECODE_TABLE will cause the alphabet validation check to correctly identify 'W' as not being in the alphabet, allowing it to be used as a padding character.
experiment_rationale: The fix diff shows the removal of the value '32' from the HEX_DECODE_TABLE array. This confirms that the table was initialized with an incorrect value that caused the validation logic to fail.
reasoning_summary: The bug is caused by an incorrect constant value in a static initialization table. The fix is a simple removal of this incorrect value, which fits the definition of Assignment/Initialization perfectly. It is not a Checking bug because the validation logic itself is correct; it is not an Algorithm/Method bug because the procedure is correct; it is not a Function/Class/Object bug because it is a local data initialization error.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: The validation logic (isInAlphabet) is correct; the problem is that the data table it relies on contains an incorrect value.
The procedure for validation is correct; the issue is purely the incorrect initialization of the lookup table.
